# Japan's Economic Profile

Japan, with a population of 125 million people, maintains the world's third-largest economy with a GDP of $4.2 trillion, though it is experiencing modest growth at 1.1% annually with an unemployment rate of just 2.6% and inflation at 3.2%. The country boasts impressive social indicators, including a life expectancy of 84.6 years, while its export sector contributes $756 billion to the economy. However, Japan faces a significant fiscal challenge with a debt-to-GDP ratio of 263%, one of the highest among developed nations.
